skill.name: agent-tools
skill.description: Run 150+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation. Models: FLUX, Veo, Gemini, Grok, Claude, Seedance, OmniHuman, Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter, and many more. Use when running AI apps, generating images/videos, calling LLMs, web search, or automating Twitter. Triggers: inference.sh, infsh, ai model, run ai, serverless ai, ai api, flux, veo, claude api, image generation, video generation, openrouter, tavily, exa search, twitter api, grok
skill.summary: Execute 150+ AI models and tools via inference.sh CLI without local GPU requirements.
skill.author: inference-shell

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 194 patterns that are false positives. The detected 'shell execution' patterns are markdown documentation examples (code blocks), not executable code. The 'pipe to shell' pattern is the standard CLI installer used by many legitimate tools. URLs are official inference.sh domains. API key access is documented CI/CD practice. No malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: External API Key Usage
low_findings.description: The skill documents using INFSH_API_KEY environment variable for CI/CD authentication. This is standard practice but requires users to protect their API keys.

content.user_title: Run 150+ AI Models with inference.sh CLI
content.value_statement: Access cloud-based AI models without local GPU requirements. Execute image generation, video creation, LLMs, and search tools through a unified CLI interface.
actual_capabilities: Generate images using FLUX, Gemini, Grok, and Stable Diffusion models
Create videos with Veo, Seedance, Wan, and OmniHuman
Call LLMs including Claude, Gemini, Kimi via OpenRouter
Perform web search with Tavily and Exa
Automate Twitter/X posts, likes, follows, and DMs
limitations: Requires inference.sh account and API authentication
Cloud-based execution means output URLs must be downloaded separately
Subject to inference.sh quota and credit limits
CLI must be installed locally before use

use_cases.title: AI-Powered Content Creation
use_cases.description: Generate marketing images, product videos, and social media content using cloud AI models without local hardware investment.

use_cases.title: Rapid AI Prototyping
use_cases.description: Test multiple AI models for a task before committing to a specific provider or building custom infrastructure.

use_cases.title: Automated Research Workflows
use_cases.description: Combine web search, LLM analysis, and content generation in automated research and reporting pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image using FLUX with this prompt: a cat astronaut floating in space, landscape orientation
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick image generation for presentations or social media

prompt_templates.title: Video Creation Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 5-second video using Veo 3.1 Fast showing a drone flying over a forest at sunset
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating short video clips for content

prompt_templates.title: LLM Query via OpenRouter
prompt_templates.prompt: Call Claude Sonnet 4.5 through OpenRouter to explain quantum computing in simple terms for a beginner
prompt_templates.scenario: Accessing LLMs without direct API integration

prompt_templates.title: Web Search and Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for the latest AI news using Tavily, then summarize the top 3 results in bullet points

output_examples.input: infsh app run falai/flux-dev-lora --input '{"prompt": "a cat astronaut"}'
infsh app run google/veo-3-1-fast --input '{"prompt": "drone over mountains"}'

output_examples.output: Search results returned with 5 relevant articles about AI trends, including summaries and source URLs

best_practices: Save API outputs to local files for offline access and backup
Use --no-wait flag for long-running tasks like video generation
Generate sample inputs first with 'infsh app sample' to understand required schema
anti_patterns: Do not commit INFSH_API_KEY to version control or share in public repositories
Avoid running apps without checking input schema first using 'infsh app get'
Do not use --no-wait for quick tasks where you need immediate output

faq.question: How do I install the inference.sh CLI?
faq.answer: Run 'curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh' then 'infsh login' to authenticate. The installer downloads the correct binary for your OS and architecture.

faq.question: Is the inference.sh CLI safe to install?
faq.answer: Yes. The installer verifies SHA-256 checksums and supports Sigstore signature verification if cosign is installed. No elevated permissions or background processes are required.

faq.question: What happens if I run out of credits?
faq.answer: You will receive a 'quota exceeded' error. Check your account balance on the inference.sh dashboard and add credits to continue using the service.

faq.question: Can I use this skill in CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes. Set the INFSH_API_KEY environment variable in your CI/CD system and the CLI will use it for authentication instead of interactive login.

faq.question: How do I check the status of a long-running task?
faq.answer: Use 'infsh task get <task-id>' to check status. Add --json for machine-readable output or --save to save results to a file.

faq.question: What AI models are available through inference.sh?
faq.answer: 150+ models including FLUX for images, Veo for video, Claude/Gemini via OpenRouter, Tavily and Exa for search, and specialized tools for 3D generation and Twitter automation.
